title: Extra-Earthy Canadian Beef Tagine
description: A hearty, peppery, one-pot Canadian dish featuring beef, onions, and dried cranberries, all served in a flavorful beef broth.
instructions: Heat butter in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add beef and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add onion and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add beef broth, cranberries, peppercorns, thyme, and flour, and stir to combine.
Cover the pot and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake at 300°F (150°C) for 2 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh thyme if desired.
To make the en-croute, roll out the puff pastry to a thickness of 1/4 inch (6 mm).
Cut the pastry into strips, about 6 inches (15 cm) wide and 18 inches (45 cm) long.
Weave the pastry strips into a lattice pattern on top of the tagine, pressing the edges to seal.
Brush the pastry with egg wash and bake at 400°F (200°C) for 25 minutes, or until golden brown.
